_meta.description: Futurpreneur Canada — financing and mentorship for entrepreneurs aged 18-39. One of the most accessible and well-known programs for young Quebec entrepreneurs.

plain_language_summary: Futurpreneur provides up to $75,000 in startup financing to young entrepreneurs aged 18-39, paired with 2 years of mentorship from an experienced business person. It's a loan (not a grant), but with better terms than a bank. The mentorship is often the most valuable part — your mentor has gone through what you're about to face.

amount.structure: Futurpreneur provides up to $20,000 directly. If you need more, the BDC matches up to $55,000 (total $75,000).

interest_rate.futurpreneur_portion: Prime rate + 1% (roughly 5-6% in 2026 — verify current rate)

repayment.interest_only_period: First year is interest-only payments — lower burden while starting up
repayment.full_repayment: Principal repayment begins year 2, over up to 5 years total

mentorship.format: Monthly meetings with a volunteer mentor (experienced entrepreneur or executive)
mentorship.plain_language: The mentor match is genuinely valuable — not just a checkbox. Many Futurpreneur alumni say the mentorship was more valuable than the money.

age.disqualifier: Must be under 40 at time of application
age.scoring: Full score if 18-39, zero if outside range

immigration_status.note: Work permit holders are generally NOT eligible — this is a hard requirement
immigration_status.future_eligibility: If you're on a work permit, you become eligible once you receive permanent residency. Futurpreneur is worth applying to as soon as your PR comes through.

eligible: Recently launched (within 12 months of first sale)
ineligible: Businesses operating for more than 12-18 months (check current rules)
Buying an existing business (acquisition financing — different program)

what_they_look_for: Clear description of the business and product/service
business_plan.free_help: Futurpreneur provides free business plan templates and advisors to help you prepare

location.montreal_note: Futurpreneur has an active Montreal presence — local advisors available

sector.eligible: Most sectors except cannabis, adult entertainment, real estate speculation

what_you_need_to_apply: Completed business plan (template available on Futurpreneur website)
Personal credit history (they check credit but low scores don't automatically disqualify)
Proof of age and eligibility (ID + immigration status)
NEQ or proof of business registration (or intent to register)

timeline.note: If approved, funds can take another 2-4 weeks to arrive after signing

common_mistakes: Applying after your business is too established (12+ months) — apply early
Not preparing a proper business plan — the quality of your plan significantly affects approval
Work permit holders applying — unfortunately not eligible until PR
Underestimating the mentorship value — engage your mentor actively
Not using the free advisor help before applying — they know what reviewers want to see

who_this_is_best_for.best_match: Young (18-39) Canadian citizen or PR launching a new business who needs startup capital and could benefit from mentorship

persona_fit.Yara: Strong match — age, immigrant (if PR), new food business
persona_fit.Marcus: Strong match — age 23, citizen, new consulting business
persona_fit.Fatima: Age 41 — may be just outside the range. Verify exact cutoff.

government_body: Futurpreneur Canada (non-profit, funded by BDC and federal government)
montreal_office: Contact through national website for Quebec advisor referral
